issuesFound.title: html element missing lang attribute
issuesFound.explanation: The root html element does not have a lang attribute, so screen readers cannot automatically select the correct speech synthesis language. This forces users to listen to mispronounced content or manually switch languages.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2: <html lang="en">

issuesFound.title: hero image missing alt attribute
issuesFound.explanation: The decorative dashboard image inside the hero section lacks an alt attribute entirely. Even though the container is aria-hidden, the missing attribute causes some validators and assistive technologies to announce the filename.
issuesFound.fix: Line 138:

issuesFound.title: page title is empty
issuesFound.explanation: The title element contains no text, leaving the page without an identifiable name in browser tabs, bookmarks, and screen-reader page lists. Users cannot quickly confirm they are on the correct page.
issuesFound.fix: Line 7: <title>Luminary Studio — Accessible design and productivity tools</title>

issuesFound.title: logo image missing alt attribute
issuesFound.explanation: The Nova Health logo image has no alt attribute

issuesFound.title: HTML lang attribute missing
issuesFound.explanation: The <html> element lacks a lang attribute, preventing screen readers from identifying the page's primary language. This affects pronunciation and braille translation for users relying on assistive technology.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2: <html lang="en">

issuesFound.title: Page title missing
issuesFound.explanation: The <title> element is empty, providing no context about the page content in browser tabs or screen reader announcements. Users cannot distinguish this page from others in their history or bookmarks.
issuesFound.fix: Line 10: <title>Luminary Studio — Accessible design and productivity tools</title>

issuesFound.title: Image missing alt attribute
issuesFound.explanation: The element displaying the hero dashboard has no alt attribute, making it invisible to screen reader users. Decorative or informative images must have alt text (even if empty) to meet WCAG 1.1.1.
issuesFound.fix: Line 126:

issuesFound.title: Non-descriptive link text
issuesFound.explanation: The link text "Read more" provides no context about the destination without surrounding information. Users scanning links list will not know what they are reading more about.
issuesFound.fix: Line 113: Start free trial (no credit card required)

issuesFound.title: Button missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The navigation toggle button contains only an icon with aria-hidden="true" and no text or aria-label. Screen reader users cannot identify the button's purpose (opening the menu).
issuesFound.fix: Line 35: <button type="button" class="nav-toggle" aria-label="Toggle navigation menu">

issuesFound.title: Toggle button missing aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: This button controls the visibility of the FAQ answer (faq-2-answer) but lacks aria-expanded to indicate its current state. Screen reader users cannot know if the content is hidden or visible.
issuesFound.fix: Line 633: <button type="button" aria-controls="faq-2-answer" aria-expanded="false" id="faq-2-question">

issuesFound.title: Form input missing accessible label
issuesFound.explanation: The newsletter email input has no associated <label>, aria-label, or aria-labelledby. Screen reader users will not know what information is expected in this field.
issuesFound.fix: Line 736: <input type="email" id="newsletter-email" name="email" autocomplete="email" required aria-required="true" placeholder="you@example.com" aria-label="Email address for newsletter">

issuesFound.title: Personal data input missing autocomplete
issuesFound.explanation: The first name input collects personal data but lacks an autocomplete attribute. This prevents browsers from assisting users in filling out the form, impacting users with cognitive or motor disabilities.
issuesFound.fix: Line 772: <input type="text" id="contact-first-name" name="first_name" autocomplete="given-name" required>

issuesFound.title: Heading level skip
issuesFound.explanation: The heading hierarchy jumps from h2 (Line 229) to h5 (Line 253), skipping levels h3 and h4. This disrupts the logical outline structure used by screen readers to navigate the page.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 253:

Connect your project

issuesFound.title: Table header missing scope
issuesFound.explanation: The <th> element "Starter" lacks a scope attribute in a table with multiple header columns. Explicit scope is required to define the header axis for assistive technology.
issuesFound.fix: Line 537: <th scope="col">Starter</th>

issuesFound.title: Broken ARIA reference
issuesFound.explanation: The section references aria-labelledby="features-heading", but no element with id="features-heading" exists in the document. This leaves the landmark without an accessible name.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 168:

Everything you need to ship accessible software

issuesFound.title: Landmark missing distinguishing label
issuesFound.explanation: There are multiple <nav> elements on the page, but this one lacks an aria-label or aria-labelledby. Users navigating by landmarks cannot distinguish this from other navigation regions.
issuesFound.fix: Line 32: <nav id="main-nav" aria-label="Main navigation">

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on :focus-visible in forced-colors media query
issuesFound.explanation: Inside @media (forced-colors: active), the :focus-visible selector removes outline with only outline-offset. This makes focus invisible in Windows High Contrast Mode where users most need visible focus indicators.
issuesFound.fix: Line 515: :focus-visible { outline: 3px solid CanvasText; outline-offset: 3px; }

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .header-inner > a:first-child:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The header logo link removes outline on focus-visible with only outline-offset. This link is interactive and keyboard users need to see when it receives focus.
issuesFound.fix: Line 759: .header-inner > a:first-child:focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 3px; }

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .nav-toggle:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation toggle button removes outline on focus-visible without visible replacement. This button controls navigation visibility and must show focus state.
issuesFound.fix: Line 789: .nav-toggle:focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 3px; }

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on [id$="-submenu"] li a:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: Dropdown submenu links remove outline on focus-visible with only outline-offset and border-radius. These are interactive navigation elements requiring visible focus indication.
issuesFound.fix: Line 828: [id$="-submenu"] li a:focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: -3px; }

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on #search-input:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The search input removes outline on focus-visible with outline-offset: 0. While the :focus rule has box-shadow, the :focus-visible rule overrides it with no visible indicator for keyboard users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 862: #search-input:focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-brand-primary); outline-offset: 0; }

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on #site-search button[type="submit"]:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The search submit button removes outline on focus-visible without visible replacement. This interactive button needs a visible focus indicator for keyboard users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 879: #site-search button[type="submit"]:focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 3px; }

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on nav[aria-label="Account navigation"] a:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: Account navigation links remove outline on focus-visible with only outline-offset and border-radius. These links are interactive and require visible focus states.
issuesFound.fix: Line 897: nav[aria-label="Account navigation"] a:focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 3px; }

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .feature-card a:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: Feature card links remove outline on focus-visible without visible replacement. These are interactive elements that keyboard users must be able to identify when focused.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1069: .feature-card a:focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 3px; }

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .filter-tabs button:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: Filter tab buttons remove outline on focus-visible with only outline-offset. These toggle buttons control product filtering and need visible focus indication.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1215: .filter-tabs button:focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 3px; }

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .pricing-toggle button:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: Pricing toggle buttons remove outline on focus-visible without visible replacement. These buttons switch between monthly and annual pricing views.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1324: .pricing-toggle button:focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 3px; }

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .table-wrapper:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The scrollable table wrapper removes outline on focus-visible with only outline-offset. This region is keyboard-focusable for scrolling and needs visible focus.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1418: .table-wrapper:focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 3px; }

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .faq-item dt button:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: FAQ accordion buttons remove outline on focus-visible with only outline-offset. These buttons expand/collapse content and require visible focus states.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1574: .faq-item dt button:focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: -3px; }

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .form-group input:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: Form inputs remove outline on focus-visible with outline-offset: 0. While :focus has box-shadow, :focus-visible overrides it with no visible indicator for keyboard users.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .form-group select:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: Form select elements remove outline on focus-visible with outline-offset: 0. Select dropdowns are interactive and need visible focus indication for keyboard users.

issuesFound.explanation: Form textareas remove outline on focus-visible with outline-offset: 0. Textareas are interactive input elements requiring visible focus states.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1622: .form-group textarea:focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-brand-primary); outline-offset: 0; }

issuesFound.explanation: Checkbox inputs remove outline on focus-visible with only outline-offset. Checkboxes are interactive form controls requiring visible focus indication.

issuesFound.explanation: Footer brand links remove outline on focus-visible with only outline-offset and border-radius. These navigation links need visible focus states for keyboard users.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on footer[role="contentinfo"] nav a:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: Footer navigation links remove outline on focus-visible with only outline-offset and border-radius. These are interactive links requiring visible focus indication.

issuesFound.explanation: Social media links remove outline on focus-visible with only outline-offset and border-radius. These interactive links need visible focus states for keyboard navigation.

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size on #site-search button[type="submit"]
issuesFound.explanation: The search submit button has explicit width: 28px and height: 28px with padding: 0, resulting in a 28×28px touch target. This is below the 44×44px minimum recommended by WCAG 2.5.8.
issuesFound.fix: Line 868: #site-search button[type="submit"] { width: 44px; height: 44px; }

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size on .btn-sm
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn-sm class has min-height: 20px with padding var(--space-1-5) = 6px per side, resulting in 32px total height. This is below the 44px minimum touch target size.
issuesFound.fix: Line 707: .btn-sm { min-height: 44px; padding: var(--space-3) var(--space-3-5); }

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size on .btn-icon
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn-icon class has min-width: 20px and min-height: 20px with padding var(--space-2-5) = 10px, resulting in 40px total size. This is below the 44px minimum.
issuesFound.fix: Line 719: .btn-icon { min-width: 44px; min-height: 44px; padding: var(--space-3); }

issuesFound.title: Visually-hidden utility removes content from screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The .sr-only class uses position: static, width: auto, height: auto, clip: auto, and overflow: visible. This does not hide content visually while keeping it accessible. Screen reader users will see this content visually when it should be hidden.
issuesFound.fix: Line 614: .sr-only { position: absolute; width: 1px; height: 1px; padding: 0; margin: -1px; overflow: hidden; clip: rect(0, 0, 0, 0); white-space: nowrap; border: 0; }

issuesFound.title: Negative word-spacing impairs readability
issuesFound.explanation: The body element has word-spacing: -0.2em which pulls words closer together. This reduces readability for users with cognitive disabilities, dyslexia, or low vision who need clear word separation.
issuesFound.fix: Line 259: body { word-spacing: 0; }

issuesFound.title: Link underline removed with no alternative visual differentiator
issuesFound.explanation: All links have text-decoration: none applied globally without an alternative non-colour visual differentiator. Users with colour vision deficiencies cannot distinguish links from surrounding text by colour alone.
issuesFound.fix: Line 295: a { text-decoration: underline; }

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override for step connector line
issuesFound.explanation: The .steps-list > .step + .step::before pseudo-element has a gradient background creating a visual connecting line with spatial properties. There is no @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) rule to disable this for users who prefer reduced motion.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1136: @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .steps-list > .step + .step::before { background: var(--colour-border); } }

issuesFound.title: mobileNav toggle functions do not update aria-expanded on trigger
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav open() and close() functions toggle the visibility of the navigation drawer using CSS classes and the hidden attribute on the overlay, but they do not update the aria-expanded state on the trigger button (_trigger). Screen reader users will not be informed when the menu opens or closes.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 1685: setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, true);
  Line 1705: setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, false);

issuesFound.title: mobileNav trigger missing initial aria-expanded and aria-controls
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav init() function does not set the initial aria-expanded="false" state on the trigger button, nor does it link the trigger to the drawer using aria-controls. This prevents assistive technology from understanding the relationship and initial state.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1740: setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false'); setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.CONTROLS, _drawer.id);

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu toggle functions do not update aria-expanded on trigger
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdownMenu _openInstance() and _closeInstance() functions manage the panel visibility but fail to update the aria-expanded attribute on the trigger button. This breaks the disclosure pattern for screen reader users.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 1875: setAttr(inst.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, true);
  Line 1890: setAttr(inst.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, false);

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu trigger missing initial aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdownMenu register() function sets aria-haspopup and aria-controls but does not initialize aria-expanded="false" on the trigger. The initial state is ambiguous to assistive technology.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1995: setAttr(trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');

issuesFound.title: siteSearch combobox does not update aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The siteSearch _open() and _close() functions show and hide the suggestion listbox but do not update aria-expanded on the search input (_inputEl). The combobox state is not communicated to screen readers.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 2215: setAttr(_inputEl, ARIA.EXPANDED, true);
  Line 2225: setAttr(_inputEl, ARIA.EXPANDED, false);

issuesFound.title: siteSearch input missing initial aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The siteSearch init() function configures the input as a combobox but does not set the initial aria-expanded="false" state. The initial closed state is not announced.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2260: setAttr(_inputEl, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');

issuesFound.title: productFilter tabs do not update aria-pressed or aria-selected
issuesFound.explanation: The productFilter _syncTabAriaStates() function updates the visual active state of filter tabs but does not update aria-pressed (for buttons) or aria-selected. The current filter selection is not accessible.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2465: setAttr(btn, ARIA.PRESSED, String(isActive));

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion open function does not update aria-expanded to true
issuesFound.explanation: The faqAccordion open() function expands the answer panel but does not set aria-expanded="true" on the trigger button. The close() function correctly sets it to false, creating an inconsistent state.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2995: setAttr(item.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'true');

issuesFound.title: inlineValidator markInvalid does not set aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: The srUtils.markInvalid() function links the error message via aria-describedby but fails to set aria-invalid="true" on the field. Screen readers may not announce the field as invalid.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1385: field.setAttribute('aria-invalid', 'true');

issuesFound.title: inlineValidator markValid does not clear aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: The srUtils.markValid() function removes the error description but does not remove or reset the aria-invalid attribute to "false". The field may remain stuck in an invalid state for assistive technology.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1395: field.setAttribute('aria-invalid', 'false');

issuesFound.title: productFilter applyFilters does not announce result count
issuesFound.explanation: The productFilter applyFilters() function updates the visible product grid and count badge but does not announce the change to an aria-live region. Screen reader users are not informed that the results have changed.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2415: liveRegion.announce(`${totalMatches} product${totalMatches !== 1 ? 's' : ''} found`);

issuesFound.title: scrollToTop click action not announced
issuesFound.explanation: The scrollToTop _onClick() function scrolls the page and moves focus but does not announce the action via a live region. Users may be disoriented by the sudden viewport change.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1495: liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.scrolledToTop || 'Scrolled to top of page');

issuesFound.title: cookieConsent banner state change not announced
issuesFound.explanation: The cookieConsent _hideBanner() and _showBanner() functions change the visibility of the consent dialog but do not announce the state change. Users may not know the banner has appeared or been dismissed.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 3775: liveRegion.announce('Cookie banner dismissed');
  Line 3795: liveRegion.announce('Cookie consent banner displayed');

issuesFound.title: mobileNav trigger missing aria-controls attribute
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav init() function fails to associate the trigger button with the navigation drawer using aria-controls. The relationship between the control and the region is missing.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1740: setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.CONTROLS, _drawer.id);

issuesFound.title: Button component spinner missing aria-hidden
issuesFound.explanation: The decorative spinner span inside the button is exposed to assistive technology without an accessible name, causing confusion. It should be hidden as it is purely visual.
issuesFound.fix: Line 273: <span className="btn-spinner" aria-hidden="true" />

issuesFound.title: Button component missing aria-busy
issuesFound.explanation: When the button is in a loading state (isLoading), it does not communicate this via aria-busy. Users are not informed that the button is temporarily unavailable due to processing.
issuesFound.fix: Line 262: <button type={type} disabled={isDisabled} aria-disabled={isDisabled || undefined} aria-busy={isLoading || undefined} className={['btn', `btn--${variant}`, `btn--${size}`, className].filter(Boolean).join(' ')} {...rest}>

issuesFound.title: Spinner component inner ring missing aria-hidden
issuesFound.explanation: The visual ring inside the Spinner component is exposed to assistive technology. Since the parent has role="status" and a label, this decorative child should be hidden.
issuesFound.fix: Line 312: <span className="spinner__ring" aria-hidden="true" />

issuesFound.title: StarRating inner spans missing aria-hidden
issuesFound.explanation: The individual star spans are exposed to assistive technology despite the parent container having the accessible label. This causes verbosity (e.g., "star, star, star").
issuesFound.fix: Line 220: <span key={i} className={filled ? 'star star--filled' : 'star star--empty'} aria-hidden="true">

issuesFound.title: Modal overlay has invalid aria-modal
issuesFound.explanation: aria-modal="true" is placed on the overlay div instead of the dialog element. This attribute is only valid on the element with role="dialog".
issuesFound.fix: Line 1475: <div className="modal-overlay" onClick={onClose}>

issuesFound.title: Modal dialog missing aria-modal
issuesFound.explanation: The element with role="dialog" is missing aria-modal="true", which is required to indicate that content outside the dialog is inert.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1478: <div ref={dialogRef} role="dialog" aria-modal="true" aria-labelledby={titleId} aria-describedby={description ? descId : undefined} className={['modal-dialog', `modal-dialog--${size}`].join(' ')} onClick={(ev) => ev.stopPropagation()}>

issuesFound.title: FormGroup required marker missing aria-hidden
issuesFound.explanation: The visual asterisk indicating a required field is exposed to assistive technology. It should be hidden to avoid announcing "star" or "asterisk" unnecessarily.

issuesFound.title: PlanCard CTA link missing specific accessible name
